frameworks.name: linear evidence integration
frameworks.one_line: Sensory evidence linearly added over space then integrated over time

frameworks.name: perceptual decision-making
frameworks.one_line: Extends simple decision integration framework to complex object recognition

frameworks.name: object recognition
frameworks.one_line: Decision read-out of object representations over prolonged timescales

core_question: How do decision-making processes integrate sensory evidence across space and time for face categorization?
key_assumptions: Face categorization can be decomposed into separable spatial and temporal integration stages
Decision read-out operates over prolonged time scales on object representations
Task-relevant features carry quantifiable momentary evidence
manipulations: Stochastic multifeature face stimuli with controlled spatiotemporal information
Quantitative model comparison against human behavior
key_findings: Evidence from task-relevant features is linearly added over space into aggregated momentary evidence
Momentary evidence is linearly integrated over time with minimum information loss
Discrimination along different category boundaries is implemented by adjusting spatial feature weights
interpretations: A flexible linear integration process over space and time bridges simple perceptual decisions to complex object recognition
Object recognition combines elaborate sensory transformation with decision-making read-out
